page.title: hary_fate_sector T Files
page.t: Discovered only two years ago by an Argon long-range survey expedition, Fate appeared at first to be little more than an unremarkable frontier sector at the edge of known space. Yet the deeper the scanners probed, the stranger the region became. Unstable readings, drifting debris of unknown origin, and a silence where no silence should exist gave the sector an unsettling reputation among explorers. Though the Argon were first to chart its gates and map its stars, they never laid formal claim to it. Some spoke of poor strategic value, others of limited resources, but among pilots and captains another explanation endures: that Fate does not wish to belong to anyone. Since its discovery, the sector has remained unclaimed, suspended between opportunity and omen, waiting for those bold enough to decide what its future will be.
